content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN markers. There is a cap on each marker. For each valid iii, the iii-th marker has colour aia_iai​. Initially, for each valid iii, the colour of the cap on the iii-th marker is also aia_iai​.

Chef wants to rearrange the caps in such a way that no marker has the same colour as its cap. (Obviously, each marker must have exactly one cap.) Can he do that? If he can, find one such way to rearrange the caps. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:

If Chef cannot successfully rearrange the caps, print a single line containing the string `"No"` (without quotes).
Otherwise, print two lines. The first line should contain the string `"Yes"` (without quotes). The second line should contain NNN space-separated integers describing a valid rearrangement of caps. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should denote the final colour of the cap on the iii-th marker.
